course_description:
Honours students within the School of Biomedical Sciences will undertake their own original research project under the guidance of an academic supervisor based at the School or affiliated Institutes. Students will learn and be assessed on their scientific communication skills in both oral and written presentations of their research project.

In this 'Biomedical Science Research Project', you will undertake a research project in an area of biomedical science of your own choosing and interest. You will conduct your research through a long placement in a world-class research environment under supervision of an academic researcher. You will develop your ability to critically analyse the scientific literature, and to conceive, design, and conduct experiments independently. You will develop your skills to assess and articulate the significance of your scholarly work, and to communicate your scientific findings through written submissions and oral presentations. Assessment items include a written Research Proposal, Thesis (Research Report),ﾠResearch Outcomes Seminar, and a Supervisor’s Report.

restrictions: Restricted to students enrolled in BBiomedSc(Hons); or BSc(Hons) or BAdvSc(Hons) majoring in Biomedical Science or Cell Biology only.

course_contacts.name: Student and Academic Administration Team
course_contacts.notes: The SBMS Student and Academic Administration Team is located on Level 1 of the Sir William Macgregor Building (64-130).

timetable:
Additional timetable information
The placement will commence in Week 6 your first semester and continue throughout the entire second semester. The placement represents a full-time commitment for this period of time.
course_aims: The course will develop skills that are valued by employers across a range of professions and industries that are possible career destinations for Biomedical Science graduates. These skills include critical analysis, scientific creativity, problem solving, technical expertise, and effective communication. This course lays the foundation for students wishing to progress to Higher Degree by Research (HDR) studies and contributes to generic skill development and job readiness for a broad range of other career options.

learning_outcomes.description: Read and critically analyse current scientific literature related to the area of the research project.
Identify research questions, investigate appropriate methodology and plan experiments to address these questions.
Perform practical and/or theoretical experimental work, under the guidance of an experienced supervisor.
Record the planning and outcome of experiments accurately and at a level that is both appropriate and expected in modern day scientific research.
Write a substantial report based on your scientific investigation in a format suitable for a biomedical science audience.
Critically discuss scientific data in the context of existing literature, draw conclusions from such discussions and suggest further experiments.
Present the outcomes of a scientific project and its significance to both expert and non-expert audiences, accurately and appropriately.

assessment_details.title: Research Proposal
assessment_details.task_description: A report comprised of a literature review relevant to the student's area of research, a statement of hypotheses and aims and a description of the proposed research plan. Students should also clearly outline the significance of their proposed research. Please consult your Honours Handbook for further information. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct . To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. As this assessment is marked by two or more examiners and a post-marking moderation process is applied, re-marks are not permitted as per the Assessment Procedure (clause 114). Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
An electronic copy of your assignment in BOTH Word and PDF format must be submitted through the Turnitin link on the course Blackboard website by the assessment due date.
You must submit your Assessment task, with the electronic coversheet available on the School's website (under Undergraduate - Assessment), to Turnitin by the submission deadline. You may submit drafts to Turnitin up to the due date, but you will only be able to submit once on or after the due date. You should also keep an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
Upon submission of the Research Proposal, you should check in the Turnitin Assignment Inbox page that your proposal was submitted successfully.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
A maximum of 14 days extension is in place to ensure students stay aligned with the research timeline, and supports timely feedback within the Honours program schedule.
Requests for extension must be made prior to the submission deadline, even if you are awaiting your supporting documentation. Late requests for extension will not be accepted for consideration unless you provide evidence of exceptional circumstances preventing you from submitting a request prior to the deadline, e.g., due to hospitalisation.
If you have been granted an extension, then the assessment specified penalty listed under Late Submission will be applied to submissions made after the due date of the approved extension.
Note that if you hold a Student Access Plan (SAP) , you will need to submit your request under those grounds and you should not apply for a discretionary extension as per UQ policy.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
If you are experiencing technical difficulties uploading your submission, please email a copy of your assessment to sbms@enquire.uq.edu.au so this can be logged on your behalf before the due date.

assessment_details.title: Research Progress Meeting
assessment_details.weight: Formative, but contributes to Supervisor's Report
assessment_details.task_description: Students are required to organise and attend a semi-formal progress meeting with members of their supervisory team prior to, or during, the first teaching week of their second semester. Ahead of this meeting, students must prepare a concise progress update using the PowerPoint template provided via Blackboard. This presentation should clearly summarise project aims, work completed to date, preliminary findings (where applicable), challenges encountered, and a proposed plan for the next phase of the project. During the meeting, students are expected to actively contribute to discussion, demonstrate depth of understanding of their project, and respond thoughtfully to feedback and questions from supervisors. At the conclusion of the meeting, the student and supervisory team fill out a Meeting Record that captures key discussion points, agreed actions, timelines and any revisions to project direction. This table is to be included in your ppt file. If you or your supervisor have concerns that your project cannot be completed satisfactorily with remaining time and resources, please schedule a discussion with course co-ordinators for advice. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The completed presentation, coverted to PDF, is to be posted to the course Blackboard site by the end of teaching week 1 of your second semester.
The finalised presentation should be circulated to all your supervisors.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
If you are unable to arrange an appropriate time with your supervisor(s) at any time prior to the deadline, please contact the Course Coordinator.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.weight: 55% Draft is formative but contributes to Supervisor's Report

Ask your supervisor(s) for feedback. You will not receive feedback from the course coordinators. This is a formative assessment item (does not contribute to your final grade) but it is highly recommended that you challenge yourself to meet the draft deadline.
assessment_details.task_description: A substantial report on the research project is to be submitted. The written research report is a document which addresses the background, rationale, methods used and outcomes of the research project. The significance of the research project findings needs to be discussed in detail. All research data for research projects associated with the project is to be stored within a UQ Research Data Manager ( UQRDM ). Please consult your Honours Handbook for further information. Any substantial timescale disruptions (i.e. greater than 3 weeks) due to extenuating circumstances (as outlined in the Honours Handbook) should be discussed with the coordinators. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct . To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. As this assessment is marked by two or more examiners and a post-marking moderation process is applied, re-marks are not permitted as per the Assessment Procedure (clause 114). assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
An electronic copy of your Research Report in BOTH Word and PDF format must be submitted through the Turnitin link on the course Blackboard website by the assessment due date.
Use the electronic coversheet available on the School's website. You may submit drafts to Turnitin up to the due date, but you will only be able to submit once on or after the due date. You should also keep an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
Upon submission of the Research Proposal, you should check in the Turnitin Assignment Inbox page that your proposal was submitted successfully.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
Discretionary extensions are not available for this task.
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extensions are not required for the DRAFT submission, if you are unable to submit by the draft due date, please discuss with your supervisor(s).
A maximum of 14 days extension is in place for the FINAL submission to minimise overlap with the Research Outcomes Seminar assessment and ensure students stay aligned with the research timeline.
It is anticipated that you will have both scheduled and unscheduled interruptions throughout the 8 months that you will be working towards the completion of your Honours Research Report and Outcome Seminar. Extension requests for the Research Report will only be considered if the interruption occurred between the draft submission date and final submission date of the Research Report.
Requests for extension must be made prior to the submission deadline, even if you are awaiting your supporting documentation. Late requests for extension will not be accepted for consideration unless you provide evidence of exceptional circumstances preventing you from submitting a request prior to the deadline, e.g., due to hospitalisation.
If you have been granted an extension, then the assessment specified penalty listed under Late Submission will be applied to submissions made after the due date of the approved extension.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
If you are experiencing technical difficulties uploading your submission, please email a copy of your assessment to sbms@enquire.uq.edu.au so this can be logged on your behalf before the due date.

assessment_details.title: Research Outcomes Seminar
assessment_details.task_description: Students will give a 15-minute presentation on the current outcomes and conclusions from their research project followed by up to 10 minutes of in-depth questioning by your examiners, other academic staff and students. Please consult your Honours Handbook for further information. All seminars will be recorded. The Associate Dean (Academic) may waive the recording of an assessment performance as outlined in the Assessment Procedure (clause 96). If required i.e for commercial in confidence projects or those involving sensitive human anatomy projects, this should be organised by the primary supervisor with the outcome then communicated to the course coordinator. Completion of the Research Outcomes Seminar is independent of any other assessment item. As this assessment is marked by two or more examiners and a post-marking moderation process is applied, re-marks are not permitted as per the Assessment Procedure (clause 114). This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct . Read full task description Hide full task description

Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
Discretionary extensions are not available for this task.
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
It is anticipated that you will have both scheduled and unscheduled interruptions throughout the 8 months you will be working towards completion of your Honours Research Report and Outcome Seminar. Extension requests for the Outcome Seminar will only be considered if the interruption occurred between the submission date of the Research Report and the Outcome Seminar date.
Requests for extension must be made prior to the submission deadline, even if you are awaiting your supporting documentation. Late requests for extension will not be accepted for consideration unless you provide evidence of exceptional circumstances preventing you from submitting a request prior to the deadline, e.g., due to hospitalisation.
If you have been granted an extension, then the assessment specified penalty listed under Late Submission will be applied to submissions made after the due date of the approved extension.

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .
learning_resources.other_course_materials: If we've listed something under further requirement, you'll need to provide your own. Required Item Description Further Requirement Scientific Literature: There is no required textbook for this course. Students are responsible for the collection of current literature in their field using the UQ Library services. The initial literature search and review will be guided by the student’s supervisor(s). Personal Protective Equipment (PPE): Minimum requirement will be fully enclosed shoes. Additional requirements will be specific to the student's host laboratory and/or research environment. Examples of such requirements include a laboratory coat and safety glasses. Students are required to check with their primary supervisor as to appropriate PPE requirements for their specific research environment and students must comply with this advice. OH&S Training (OHS): You will, at a minimum, be required to complete the relevant Induction(s) and OH&S training for the School, Institute, Industry and/or Hospital environment(s) in which you will be working. Additional training may be required to operate equipment or perform experimental tasks associated with completion of your research project. Your primary supervisor will advise you of the need for additional (OHS) training where applicable. Ethics Training: Students engaged with animal research as part of their project must also complete online ethics training, attend local animal house inductions and technical skill development workshops, as required for their project. Students engaged with projects involving human subjects and/or data must similarly complete relevant online training and familiarise themselves with ethical requirements / obligations specific to their project and/or as directed by their supervisor. Recommended Item Description Further Requirement UQ Library Courses: Participation in professional development courses on the use of Databases and Endnote is encouraged.

learning_activities.topic: Orientation Session All students are expected to attend this on campus orientation session at 10:00am on Monday of teaching Week 1. Location to be confirmed. Learning outcomes: L08

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 6 To Week 9 (30 Mar - 03 May)
learning_activities.topic: Literature review and research proposal writing Students will undertake a self-directed literature search and prepare the Research Proposal assessment item under guidance of their supervisor. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L08

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 6 To Week 24 (30 Mar - 18 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: Independent Research Students to participate in laboratory-based research work. Results to be presented in an independent research report. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08

Research Progress Meeting Students to deliver a presentation to their entire supervisory team (if possible), likely in the context of a lab meeting. The student and supervisor(s) will consider progress, and clarify remaining work to be done, adjusting course if appropriate. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L06, L07, L08
Research Outcomes Seminar Students to deliver a presentation of the research achieved during the Honours year. Learning outcomes: L06, L07, L08

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
